faq.q: What are normal fuel trim values?
faq.a: Normal fuel trims are typically within plus or minus 10%. Values beyond plus or minus 25% usually trigger a code.

faq.q: What is the difference between STFT and LTFT?
faq.a: Short-term fuel trim (STFT) is the real-time correction based on O2 sensor feedback. Long-term fuel trim (LTFT) is a learned, averaged correction that compensates for persistent conditions.

faq.q: Can a dirty air filter cause P0170?
faq.a: A dirty air filter alone rarely causes this code, but combined with a contaminated MAF sensor, it can contribute to fuel trim imbalance.

title: System Too Lean Bank 1
shortDescription: The fuel system on bank 1 is running too lean (too much air or too little fuel).
fullDescription: The PCM has detected that the long-term fuel trim on bank 1 is excessively positive, meaning it is adding more fuel than normal to compensate for a lean condition. This code is one of the most common OBD-II codes and indicates that the engine is receiving too much air relative to fuel, or not enough fuel relative to air. The PCM has reached the limit of its ability to compensate.
safetyImpact: Lean conditions can cause misfires and engine overheating under load. Prolonged lean running can damage the catalytic converter.

causes.cause: Vacuum leak (intake manifold gasket, vacuum hose, PCV valve)
Weak fuel pump or clogged fuel filter

symptoms: Engine misfires at idle or under load
Possible hissing noise from vacuum leak

fixSteps.title: Check Fuel Trims
fixSteps.instruction: Using a scan tool, compare fuel trims at idle and at 2500 RPM. If the lean condition is worse at idle, a vacuum leak is likely. If worse at higher RPM, a fuel delivery issue is more likely.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for Vacuum Leaks
fixSteps.instruction: Check all vacuum hoses, the PCV valve and hoses, intake manifold gaskets, and throttle body gasket. A smoke test is the most reliable method.

fixSteps.title: Clean or Replace MAF Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and clean the MAF sensor with MAF-specific cleaner. If cleaning does not normalize fuel trims, the sensor may need replacement.

fixSteps.title: Test Fuel Pressure
fixSteps.instruction: Check fuel pressure at idle and under load. Low pressure indicates a weak pump, clogged filter, or faulty regulator. Replace as needed.

faq.q: What is the most common cause of P0171?
faq.a: The most common cause is a vacuum leak, particularly cracked or disconnected vacuum hoses, worn intake manifold gaskets, or a leaking PCV system.

faq.q: Can P0171 cause a misfire?
faq.a: Yes, a lean mixture can cause misfires, especially at idle. You may also see P0300 (random misfire) or cylinder-specific misfire codes alongside P0171.

faq.q: Why do I have P0171 and P0174 together?
faq.a: P0171 (bank 1) and P0174 (bank 2) together indicate a system-wide lean condition, pointing to a common cause like a MAF sensor issue, fuel pump problem, or large vacuum leak.

title: System Too Rich Bank 1
shortDescription: The fuel system on bank 1 is running too rich (too much fuel or too little air).
fullDescription: The PCM has detected that the long-term fuel trim on bank 1 is excessively negative, meaning it is removing fuel to compensate for a rich condition. This indicates the engine is receiving too much fuel relative to air. Rich conditions waste fuel, increase emissions, can foul spark plugs, and can damage the catalytic converter through overheating.
safetyImpact: Rich exhaust can overheat the catalytic converter, potentially creating a fire hazard in extreme cases. Fuel economy is reduced.

causes.cause: High fuel pressure from faulty regulator
Saturated charcoal canister purging excess fuel vapor
Faulty O2 sensor causing incorrect rich correction

fixSteps.title: Check Fuel Trims
fixSteps.instruction: Record STFT and LTFT on bank 1 using a scan tool. Negative values confirm the PCM is actively reducing fuel to compensate for a rich condition.

fixSteps.title: Test Fuel Pressure
fixSteps.instruction: Connect a fuel pressure gauge and compare to specification. If pressure is too high, the fuel pressure regulator may be faulty or the return line restricted.

fixSteps.title: Check Injectors
fixSteps.instruction: Perform an injector balance test or check for fuel dripping from injectors with the engine off and fuel system pressurized. Check spark plugs for signs of a single rich cylinder.

fixSteps.title: Inspect EVAP System
fixSteps.instruction: Check the charcoal canister purge valve. A stuck-open purge valve can flood the intake with fuel vapor. Disconnect the purge line and see if fuel trims improve.

faq.q: Can a bad oxygen sensor cause P0172?
faq.a: Yes, a faulty upstream O2 sensor that falsely reads lean will cause the PCM to add fuel, creating an actual rich condition and setting P0172.

faq.q: Will P0172 damage my catalytic converter?
faq.a: Yes, excess fuel that reaches the catalytic converter can cause it to overheat and eventually fail, which is a much more expensive repair.

faq.q: What does black smoke from the exhaust mean?
faq.a: Black smoke indicates unburned fuel being expelled through the exhaust, confirming a rich fuel condition.

title: System Too Lean Bank 2
shortDescription: The fuel system on bank 2 is running too lean (too much air or too little fuel).
fullDescription: The PCM has detected that the long-term fuel trim on bank 2 is excessively positive, indicating a lean condition on the bank opposite cylinder 1. This code is the bank 2 equivalent of P0171. If both P0171 and P0174 are present, a system-wide issue like a failing MAF sensor or weak fuel pump is likely. If only P0174 is set, the problem is likely isolated to the bank 2 side.
safetyImpact: Lean conditions can cause misfires and engine damage under sustained load.

causes.cause: Vacuum leak on bank 2 intake runners or gasket
Dirty or failing MAF sensor (if both banks affected)
Weak fuel pump or clogged filter (if both banks affected)

fixSteps.title: Compare Bank Trims
fixSteps.instruction: Using a scan tool, compare bank 1 and bank 2 fuel trims. If only bank 2 is lean, the issue is isolated to that bank (vacuum leak, injector). If both banks are lean, look for a common cause.

fixSteps.title: Check for Vacuum Leaks
fixSteps.instruction: Focus on bank 2 intake runner gaskets, vacuum hoses on that side, and the rear of the intake manifold. Use a smoke machine for accurate leak detection.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Fuel Delivery
fixSteps.instruction: If both banks are lean, check fuel pressure and MAF sensor. If only bank 2, check individual injectors on that bank for proper flow.

fixSteps.title: Repair and Verify
fixSteps.instruction: Fix the identified leak or fuel delivery issue. Clear codes and monitor fuel trims during a test drive to verify they return to the normal range.

faq.q: Why is only bank 2 lean?
faq.a: An isolated bank 2 lean condition usually points to a vacuum leak or injector issue specific to that bank, such as a cracked intake runner or leaking gasket on the bank 2 side.

faq.q: Can lower intake manifold gaskets cause P0174?
faq.a: Yes, on many V-type engines, the lower intake manifold gasket can develop a leak that preferentially affects one bank, causing a single-bank lean code.

faq.q: If I have both P0171 and P0174, is it a bigger problem?
faq.a: It indicates a system-wide lean condition, typically from a common component like the MAF sensor, fuel pump, or fuel filter. Fix the common cause and both codes should resolve.

title: System Too Rich Bank 2
shortDescription: The fuel system on bank 2 is running too rich (too much fuel or too little air).
fullDescription: The PCM has detected that the long-term fuel trim on bank 2 is excessively negative, indicating a rich condition on bank 2. This is the bank 2 equivalent of P0172. If both P0172 and P0175 are present, a system-wide rich condition is indicated. If only P0175 is set, the issue is likely specific to bank 2, such as a leaking injector on that side.
safetyImpact: Rich exhaust can overheat the bank 2 catalytic converter. Fuel economy is reduced.

causes.cause: MAF sensor over-reporting airflow (if both banks)

fixSteps.title: Compare Bank Trims
fixSteps.instruction: Compare bank 1 and bank 2 fuel trims. If only bank 2 is rich, the issue is isolated. If both are rich, look for a system-wide cause.

fixSteps.title: Check Bank 2 Injectors
fixSteps.instruction: Pull spark plugs on bank 2 and inspect for heavy black deposits indicating a rich cylinder. Perform an injector balance or leak-down test.

fixSteps.title: Check Fuel Pressure
fixSteps.instruction: Test fuel pressure against specifications. High pressure affects all cylinders equally.

fixSteps.title: Repair and Verify
fixSteps.instruction: Address the root cause (replace leaking injector, regulator, or sensor). Clear codes and monitor fuel trims during a test drive.

faq.q: Can a leaking injector cause only one bank to be rich?
faq.a: Yes, a leaking injector on bank 2 will add excess fuel to that bank specifically, causing P0175 without affecting bank 1.

faq.q: Is P0175 worse than P0172?
faq.a: They are equally concerning since both indicate a rich condition. The difference is which bank is affected. The same urgency applies to both.

faq.q: Can engine oil affect fuel trims?
faq.a: Yes, if oil enters the combustion chamber through worn valve seals or piston rings, it adds hydrocarbon fuel to the exhaust, which the O2 sensor reads as rich.

title: Fuel Composition Sensor Malfunction
shortDescription: The fuel composition sensor circuit is reporting values outside the expected range.
fullDescription: The PCM has detected a malfunction in the fuel composition (flex fuel) sensor circuit. This sensor measures the ethanol content of the fuel to allow the PCM to adjust fuel injection and ignition timing accordingly. Vehicles equipped with this sensor can run on gasoline, E85, or any blend. A malfunction means the PCM cannot determine the fuel's ethanol content and may use default values.
safetyImpact: Incorrect fuel mixture assumptions can cause detonation (knock) or poor performance, especially with high-ethanol fuel.

causes.cause: Water in the fuel affecting sensor reading

fixSteps.title: Check Connector
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the fuel composition sensor (usually mounted inline on the fuel supply line). Inspect the electrical connector for corrosion, damage, or loose pins.

fixSteps.title: Test Sensor Signal
fixSteps.instruction: Using a scan tool, check the fuel composition reading. With regular gasoline, ethanol content should read 0-15%. With E85, it should read 70-85%.

fixSteps.title: Check Wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Test the sensor signal wire, power supply, and ground for proper voltage and continuity using a multimeter.

fixSteps.title: Replace Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the sensor readings are inaccurate or erratic, replace the fuel composition sensor. Relieve fuel pressure before disconnecting fuel lines. Clear codes and verify.

faq.q: What is a fuel composition sensor?
faq.a: It is a sensor found on flex fuel vehicles that measures the ethanol percentage in the fuel so the PCM can adjust fuel delivery and timing accordingly.

faq.q: Can I run E85 with a bad fuel composition sensor?
faq.a: It is not recommended. Without knowing the ethanol content, the PCM may use incorrect fuel maps, causing potential engine damage from detonation.

faq.q: Does my car have a fuel composition sensor?
faq.a: Only flex fuel (FFV) vehicles have this sensor. Check for an E85/FlexFuel badge on your vehicle or consult your owner's manual.

title: Fuel Temperature Sensor A Malfunction
shortDescription: The fuel temperature sensor A circuit is reporting values outside the expected range.
fullDescription: The PCM has detected a malfunction in fuel temperature sensor A circuit. The fuel temperature sensor monitors the temperature of fuel in the fuel rail or fuel line, which affects fuel density and therefore the amount of fuel injected. This data allows the PCM to compensate for fuel density changes with temperature. A malfunction can cause slight fuel delivery inaccuracies.
safetyImpact: Minimal safety impact. Fuel delivery may be slightly inaccurate but within a manageable range.

causes.cause: Open or short in sensor circuit

symptoms: Minor drivability differences in extreme temperatures
Hard starting in very hot or cold conditions

fixSteps.title: Locate Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: The fuel temperature sensor is typically located on the fuel rail or inline on the fuel supply line. Consult your service manual for the exact location.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Connector
fixSteps.instruction: Check the sensor connector for corrosion, damage, or loose connections. Clean with electrical contact cleaner if needed.

fixSteps.title: Test Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Measure the sensor resistance and compare to the manufacturer's temperature-to-resistance chart. At room temperature, typical values are 2,000-3,000 ohms.

fixSteps.title: Replace Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the sensor is out of specification, replace it. Relieve fuel system pressure before working on fuel rail-mounted sensors. Clear codes and verify.

faq.q: Is the fuel temperature sensor important?
faq.a: It provides a minor correction to fuel delivery based on fuel density. The engine will run adequately without it, using a default value.

faq.q: Can hot fuel cause drivability issues?
faq.a: Yes, very hot fuel is less dense, and without proper compensation the engine may run slightly lean. In extreme cases, fuel can vaporize in the lines, causing vapor lock.

faq.q: Is this the same as the coolant temperature sensor?
faq.a: No, the fuel temperature sensor measures fuel temperature, while the ECT sensor measures engine coolant temperature. They are separate sensors with different functions.

title: Fuel Rail Pressure Sensor Malfunction
shortDescription: The fuel rail pressure sensor circuit is reporting a signal outside the expected range.
fullDescription: The PCM has detected a malfunction in the fuel rail pressure sensor circuit. This sensor directly measures the fuel pressure in the fuel rail and is critical for precise fuel injection control, especially on gasoline direct injection (GDI) systems. A malfunctioning sensor can lead to incorrect fuel delivery, causing drivability problems ranging from hard starting to severe performance loss.
safetyImpact: Incorrect fuel pressure readings can cause severe misfires, stalling, or inability to start. On GDI systems, this is a critical sensor.

causes.cause: Actual fuel pressure problem (pump, regulator, leak)
Corroded sensor connector due to fuel vapor exposure

symptoms: Severe power loss or limp mode

fixSteps.title: Read Sensor Data
fixSteps.instruction: Using a scan tool, compare the fuel rail pressure reading to actual fuel pressure measured with a mechanical gauge. Significant discrepancy indicates a sensor issue.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Connector and Wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Check the fuel rail pressure sensor connector for fuel contamination, corrosion, or damage. Inspect the wiring harness for chafing or breaks.

fixSteps.title: Verify Fuel System
fixSteps.instruction: Test the fuel pump and check for fuel leaks to rule out an actual pressure problem that the sensor may be correctly reporting.

fixSteps.title: Replace Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the sensor is faulty, replace it. Relieve fuel system pressure first. On GDI systems, follow manufacturer torque specifications carefully. Clear codes and verify.

faq.q: Is the fuel rail pressure sensor the same as the fuel pressure regulator?
faq.a: No, the sensor reads the pressure, while the regulator controls it. They are separate components, though a faulty regulator can cause sensor-related codes.

faq.q: Can P0190 cause a no-start?
faq.a: Yes, especially on GDI systems. Without accurate fuel pressure data, the PCM cannot calculate the correct injector pulse width, making starting very difficult.

faq.q: Is this code more critical on GDI engines?
faq.a: Yes, gasoline direct injection systems operate at very high fuel pressures (2,000+ PSI) and rely heavily on this sensor for precise fuel metering.

title: Injector Circuit Malfunction
shortDescription: A malfunction has been detected in the fuel injector circuit (general, not cylinder-specific).
fullDescription: The PCM has detected a general malfunction in the fuel injector control circuit. This code is set when the PCM cannot determine which specific injector has a problem, or when a common circuit element (like a power supply or ground shared by multiple injectors) has failed. This can cause multiple cylinders to misfire or the engine to not start.
safetyImpact: Engine may run very poorly or not start. Loss of power during driving can create dangerous situations.

causes.cause: Open or shorted injector wiring harness
Failed injector driver circuit in PCM
Blown injector power supply fuse or relay
Poor ground connection for injector circuit

symptoms: Strong fuel smell if injectors stuck open
No fuel smell if injectors not firing

fixSteps.title: Check Fuse and Relay
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the fuel injector power supply fuse and relay are functioning. The injector relay provides battery voltage to all injectors. Replace if faulty.

fixSteps.title: Check Injector Power
fixSteps.instruction: With the key on, verify battery voltage is present at each injector connector. If voltage is missing at all injectors, the relay or power feed wire is the issue.

fixSteps.title: Test Individual Injectors
fixSteps.instruction: Measure the resistance of each injector (typically 12-16 ohms for high-impedance, 2-4 ohms for low-impedance). Compare to specifications. An outlier indicates a failed injector.

fixSteps.title: Check PCM and Wiring
fixSteps.instruction: If individual injectors test good, check wiring from the PCM to each injector for continuity. In rare cases, the PCM injector driver may have failed.

faq.q: What is the difference between P0200 and P0201-P0204?
faq.a: P0200 is a general injector circuit code when the PCM cannot identify the specific cylinder. P0201-P0204 identify the specific cylinder with the issue.

faq.q: Can a bad PCM cause P0200?
faq.a: Yes, but it is uncommon. The PCM's internal injector driver circuits can fail, typically one at a time. Check all wiring and injectors before condemning the PCM.

faq.q: Will P0200 cause the engine to not start?
faq.a: If the common power supply to all injectors is lost (blown fuse or bad relay), the engine will crank but not start.

title: Injector Circuit Cylinder 1
shortDescription: A malfunction has been detected in the fuel injector circuit for cylinder 1.
fullDescription: The PCM has detected a fault in the fuel injector circuit for cylinder 1. This means the PCM cannot properly control the cylinder 1 injector, either due to an electrical open, short, or the injector itself has failed. The cylinder will not receive fuel (or will receive uncontrolled fuel), causing a misfire on that cylinder.
safetyImpact: Running on fewer cylinders reduces power and can cause catalytic converter damage from unburned fuel.

causes.cause: Open or shorted wiring to cylinder 1 injector
PCM injector driver failure for cylinder 1

fixSteps.title: Check Injector Connector
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the cylinder 1 fuel injector and check that the electrical connector is fully seated. Look for corrosion or melted plastic.

fixSteps.title: Measure Injector Resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the injector and measure resistance across its terminals. Compare to specification (typically 12-16 ohms). Open circuit or very low resistance indicates a failed injector.

fixSteps.title: Test Injector Signal
fixSteps.instruction: Use a noid light connected to the injector harness while cranking. If it flashes, the PCM is sending the pulse signal. If not, check wiring to the PCM.

fixSteps.title: Replace Injector
fixSteps.instruction: If the injector has failed, replace it. Remove the fuel rail, swap the injector, use new O-rings, and reinstall. Clear codes and verify the repair.

faq.q: Will P0201 always accompany a P0301 misfire code?
faq.a: Usually, yes. If the injector circuit is not working, cylinder 1 will misfire and you will likely see both codes.

faq.q: Can I swap injectors to test?
faq.a: Yes, swapping the cylinder 1 injector with another cylinder's injector is a good diagnostic technique. If the code follows the injector, the injector is faulty.

faq.q: What is a noid light?
faq.a: A noid light is an inexpensive tool that plugs into the injector connector and flashes when the PCM sends an injection pulse. It confirms the PCM and wiring are functioning.

title: Injector Circuit Cylinder 2
shortDescription: A malfunction has been detected in the fuel injector circuit for cylinder 2.
fullDescription: The PCM has detected a fault in the fuel injector circuit for cylinder 2. The PCM monitors the injector circuit for proper electrical response when it commands the injector to fire. An open coil, short circuit, or high resistance in the circuit will prevent proper fuel delivery to cylinder 2, causing a dead misfire on that cylinder.
safetyImpact: Cylinder 2 misfire reduces power output and can damage the catalytic converter with unburned fuel.

causes.cause: Open or damaged wiring to cylinder 2 injector

fixSteps.title: Check Connector
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the cylinder 2 injector connector is secure and free of corrosion. Wiggle the connector while the engine is running to check for intermittent connection.

fixSteps.title: Test Injector Resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the injector and measure coil resistance. Compare to the other injectors and to specifications.

fixSteps.title: Verify PCM Signal
fixSteps.instruction: Use a noid light or oscilloscope to verify the PCM is sending injection pulses to cylinder 2.

fixSteps.title: Replace Injector
fixSteps.instruction: If the injector is faulty, replace it. Use new O-rings and lubricate with clean engine oil before installation. Clear codes and verify.

faq.q: Can a wiring problem mimic a bad injector?
faq.a: Yes, a broken wire or corroded connector will prevent the PCM signal from reaching the injector, producing the same symptoms as a failed injector.

faq.q: Should I replace all injectors when one fails?
faq.a: Not necessarily, but if the injectors have high mileage, consider replacing the full set to ensure balanced fuel delivery across all cylinders.

faq.q: Can P0202 be intermittent?
faq.a: Yes, a loose connector or partially broken wire can cause intermittent P0202 that comes and goes, especially during engine vibration or temperature changes.

title: Injector Circuit Cylinder 3
shortDescription: A malfunction has been detected in the fuel injector circuit for cylinder 3.
fullDescription: The PCM has detected a fault in the fuel injector circuit for cylinder 3. The injector coil may be open or shorted, the wiring may be damaged, or the connector may have a poor connection. This prevents proper fuel delivery to cylinder 3, resulting in a misfire and associated drivability symptoms.
safetyImpact: Misfire on cylinder 3 reduces power and can overheat the catalytic converter.

fixSteps.title: Check Connector
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the cylinder 3 injector connector for proper seating, corrosion, or heat damage. Ensure the locking tab is engaged.

fixSteps.title: Measure Injector Resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect and measure the injector coil resistance. It should match specifications and be similar to the other injectors.

fixSteps.title: Swap Test
fixSteps.instruction: Swap the cylinder 3 injector with a known-good injector from another cylinder. If the code follows the injector, it is faulty. If it stays on cylinder 3, the wiring is the issue.

fixSteps.title: Replace Injector or Repair Wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the faulty injector with new O-rings, or repair the damaged wiring. Clear codes and verify on a test drive.

faq.q: Is cylinder 3 harder to access than others?
faq.a: On inline engines, all injectors are equally accessible. On V-type engines, cylinder 3 may be on the rear bank which can be harder to reach.

faq.q: Can I clean a clogged injector instead of replacing it?
faq.a: P0203 is an electrical circuit code, not a flow issue. Cleaning will not fix an electrical fault. However, if you also have flow concerns, professional ultrasonic cleaning can help.

faq.q: What if the code returns after replacing the injector?
faq.a: If the code returns with a new injector, the problem is in the wiring or the PCM driver circuit. Check wiring continuity from the connector to the PCM.

title: Injector Circuit Cylinder 4
shortDescription: A malfunction has been detected in the fuel injector circuit for cylinder 4.
fullDescription: The PCM has detected a fault in the fuel injector circuit for cylinder 4. The PCM monitors the voltage and current characteristics of the injector driver circuit and has determined that the circuit for cylinder 4 is not responding as expected. This will cause a misfire on cylinder 4 and associated performance degradation.
safetyImpact: Loss of one cylinder reduces power and can damage the catalytic converter.

causes.cause: Open or shorted wiring in cylinder 4 injector circuit
PCM driver circuit failure for cylinder 4

fixSteps.title: Inspect Connector
fixSteps.instruction: Check the cylinder 4 injector connector for looseness, corrosion, or physical damage. On some engines, cylinder 4 is at the rear and may be harder to access.

fixSteps.title: Test Injector
fixSteps.instruction: Measure the cylinder 4 injector resistance. Compare to specification and to the other injectors. Also perform a buzz test with a scan tool if available.

fixSteps.title: Check Wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Verify continuity from the injector connector to the PCM. Check for shorts to ground or to power. Pay attention to areas where the harness may rub against the engine.

fixSteps.title: Replace Injector
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the faulty injector with new O-rings. Ensure proper seating in the fuel rail and intake manifold. Clear codes and verify.

faq.q: Can I drive with a dead cylinder 4?
faq.a: You can drive short distances at low speed, but avoid sustained driving. Unburned fuel from the dead cylinder can overheat and destroy the catalytic converter.

faq.q: How do I perform a bi-directional injector test?
faq.a: With an advanced scan tool, you can command individual injectors to fire. This tests both the PCM driver and the injector itself. Listen for a clicking sound at each injector.

faq.q: Is P0204 related to ignition problems?
faq.a: No, P0204 is specifically a fuel injector circuit code. Ignition issues on cylinder 4 would cause P0304 (misfire) but not P0204 unless there is also an injector problem.

title: Injector Circuit Cylinder 5
shortDescription: A malfunction has been detected in the fuel injector circuit for cylinder 5.
fullDescription: The PCM has detected a fault in the fuel injector circuit for cylinder 5. This code applies to engines with 5 or more cylinders (V6, V8, inline-5, inline-6, etc.). The PCM has detected an abnormal voltage or current response from the cylinder 5 injector driver, indicating an open circuit, short, or failed injector. Cylinder 5 will not receive proper fuel delivery.
safetyImpact: Loss of one cylinder reduces power. On V6 and V8 engines, the imbalance can stress engine mounts and the catalytic converter.

causes.cause: PCM injector driver failure for cylinder 5

fixSteps.title: Locate Cylinder 5
fixSteps.instruction: Identify cylinder 5 location using the manufacturer's cylinder numbering guide. On V-engines, it may be on the rear bank. Inspect the injector connector.

fixSteps.title: Test Injector Resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the cylinder 5 injector and measure resistance. Compare to the other injectors and to manufacturer specifications.

fixSteps.title: Check Wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Test wiring continuity from the injector harness to the PCM. On V-engines, the wiring to rear-bank injectors travels a longer path and is more susceptible to damage.

fixSteps.title: Replace Injector
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the faulty cylinder 5 injector. On some V-type engines, the upper intake manifold may need to be removed for access. Use new O-rings and clear codes.

faq.q: Does P0205 only apply to engines with 5+ cylinders?
faq.a: Yes, cylinder 5 only exists on engines with 5 or more cylinders, such as V6, V8, inline-5, inline-6, V10, etc.

faq.q: Is cylinder 5 harder to reach on a V8?
faq.a: On many V8 engines, cylinder 5 is on the rear bank near the firewall, which can make access more difficult. The upper intake manifold may need to be removed on some designs.

faq.q: Can heat damage injector wiring?
faq.a: Yes, injector wiring runs across the top of the engine and is exposed to high temperatures. Over time, insulation can crack or melt, causing shorts or open circuits.
